cards.definition: The weekly watering rate for young coffee plants once the monsoon has ended and rainfall can no longer be relied on.
cards.analogy: A weekly ration sized to a young plant's actual root system, not a fire-hose approach.
cards.why_it_matters: Under-watering at this stage risks losing a young plant right after the establishment investment has already been made.
cards.cloze: After monsoon cessation, young coffee plants should be watered with ___ litres of water per plant once a week.

cards.definition: The baseline N:P:K fertilizer dose for maintaining vegetative growth in mature coffee plants.
cards.analogy: A maintenance diet, not a growth-spurt dose — this keeps an already-established plant healthy rather than pushing rapid new growth.
cards.why_it_matters: The baseline number a farm's actual fertilizer program can be checked against.
cards.cloze: Grown-up coffee plants need a sustenance dose of ___ kg per hectare of N:P:K for vegetative growth.

cards.definition: The ceiling on how much N:P:K can be applied in a single split application, above which nutrient loss (rather than uptake) becomes likely.
cards.analogy: A per-meal portion limit — beyond this, more food isn't better absorbed, it's mostly wasted.
cards.why_it_matters: A season's full fertilizer recommendation, above this ceiling, needs to be split across multiple applications rather than dumped in one go.
cards.cloze: A single split dose of fertilizer should not exceed ___ kg per hectare of N:P:K to avoid nutrient loss.
